channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Palestinian officials hail local elections in a Gaza community and the West Bank
item.description: Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.
item.content:encoded:
Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.

(Image credit: Mahmoud Illean)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Monte Coleman, who won 3 Super Bowls as a Washington linebacker, dies at age 68
item.description: The hard-nosed linebacker, who won three Super Bowls and later coached Arkansas-Pine Bluff, has died. UAPB and the Commanders announced his death Sunday. No cause of death was disclosed.
item.content:encoded:
The hard-nosed linebacker, who won three Super Bowls and later coached Arkansas-Pine Bluff, has died. UAPB and the Commanders announced his death Sunday. No cause of death was disclosed.

(Image credit: Gregory Payan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Alleged Colorado attacker's family released after nearly a year in detention
item.description: A Texas judge ordered Hayam El Gamal and her five children released Thursday. Two days later, their lawyers say, ICE re-arrested and tried to deport them.
item.content:encoded:
A Texas judge ordered Hayam El Gamal and her five children released Thursday. Two days later, their lawyers say, ICE re-arrested and tried to deport them.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: A fast-growing Georgia wildfire tops 31 square miles, with evacuations possible
item.description: One of two large wildfires in southeastern Georgia continues to grow and now exceeds 31 square miles.
item.content:encoded:
One of two large wildfires in southeastern Georgia continues to grow and now exceeds 31 square miles.

(Image credit: Office of Gov. Brian Kemp)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A chaotic White House Correspondents' Dinner, as told by NPR reporters in the room
item.description: Top government officials were rushed out of the Washington Hilton after gunshots were heard at the dinner. NPR journalists describe hiding under tables and scrambling for information as the night unfolded.
item.content:encoded:
Top government officials were rushed out of the Washington Hilton after gunshots were heard at the dinner. NPR journalists describe hiding under tables and scrambling for information as the night unfolded.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: What we know about Cole Allen, suspected White House Correspondents' dinner shooter
item.description: The 31-year-old teacher and engineer from California sent an alleged message to family members saying that he wanted to target administration officials.
item.content:encoded:
The 31-year-old teacher and engineer from California sent an alleged message to family members saying that he wanted to target administration officials.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Lydia Calitri

item.title: Photos: The aftermath of the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
item.description: A suspect has been arrested after firing shots at a security screening area at the Washington Hilton on Saturday night.
item.content:encoded:
A suspect has been arrested after firing shots at a security screening area at the Washington Hilton on Saturday night.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Details emerge of alleged shooter at White House correspondents' dinner
item.description: The alleged gunman has been identified as Cole Allen, according to two sources familiar with the matter.
item.content:encoded:
The alleged gunman has been identified as Cole Allen, according to two sources familiar with the matter.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: How China is responding to the stalling of talks between the U.S. and Iran
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Zongyuan Zoe Liu, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, about how China views the current crisis in the Middle East
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Zongyuan Zoe Liu, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, about how China views the current crisis in the Middle East
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: World Press Photo Contest winners cast a lens on resilience, pain and bliss
item.description: A selection of prize honorees from the 2026 World Press Photo Contest capture the pain of the past year — but also focus on moments of strength, determination and joy.
item.content:encoded:
A selection of prize honorees from the 2026 World Press Photo Contest capture the pain of the past year — but also focus on moments of strength, determination and joy.

(Image credit: Ihsaan Haffejee for GroundUp)
item.dc:creator: Kamala Thiagarajan
